title: unit tests: root check
body:
The unit tests currently require running as root.  This has caused some
confusion that justifies adding a root check to `make localunit` and
error out for non-root users instead of starting the tests deemed to
fail.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> DAE find it a little alarming that unit tests won't run rootless?

It is and we need to address this. This change will make it more explicit.

/lgtm
But I would like to get some of the unit tests to run as rootless.

> /lgtm
> But I would like to get some of the unit tests to run as rootless.

I created a Jira card for that :+1:
